title: Rules for distribution

rules.condition: Surviving spouse, no issue, no parents

rules.notes: When an issue dies before the intestate, the deceased issue's share is divided evenly among their own children.

rules.condition: Surviving spouse and parents, no issue
Surviving parents, no spouse, no issue
Surviving brothers and sisters, no spouse, no issue, no parents
rules.notes: Children of a deceased sibling represent their parent to claim their share.

rules.condition: Surviving grandparents, no spouse, no issue, no parents, no siblings or children of siblings
Surviving uncles and aunts, no spouse, no issue, no parents, no siblings or children of siblings, no grandparents
No surviving relatives as defined in the rules

general_notes: Does not apply to Muslims, whose intestate estates are governed by Muslim law.
'Children' refers to legitimate children and legally adopted children. Illegitimate children and stepchildren have no legal standing under this Act.
Jointly held properties (Joint Tenancy), nominated life insurance policies, and CPF funds are generally not distributable under this Act.
